task_completion.description: Verify that the OpenBCI GUI is running and configured to use Synthetic data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI window is visible, and the data source indicator (usually at the top or in the system control panel) shows that 'Synthetic' (Cyton) data is selected.

task_completion.description: Verify that Stream 1 is configured with the LSL protocol and TimeSeries data type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Networking widget of the OpenBCI GUI, Stream 1 has its 'Protocol' dropdown set to 'LSL' and its 'Data Type' dropdown set to 'TimeSeries'.

task_completion.description: Verify that Stream 2 is configured with the OSC protocol, BandPower data type, and the correct IP/Port.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Networking widget, Stream 2 has its 'Protocol' set to 'OSC', 'Data Type' set to 'BandPower', 'IP' field set to '127.0.0.1', and 'Port' field set to '12345'.

task_completion.description: Verify that both network streams have been started.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main 'Start' button in the Networking widget has been clicked (it typically changes to 'Stop' or the UI indicates that streaming is active).

task_completion.description: Verify that a screenshot of the configuration was saved to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager window showing a screenshot file inside '/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots', or a visual notification/log in the OpenBCI GUI confirming the screenshot was saved to that path.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the genuine OpenBCI GUI application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the authentic OpenBCI GUI interface with its standard widgets, buttons, and layout, rather than a text editor or a mock-up.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use terminal commands to bypass GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing commands like 'touch' or 'convert' being used to create fake screenshot files in the target directory without actually using the GUI.

integrity.description: Verify the configuration values were actually entered into the GUI fields.
integrity.visual_evidence: The IP, Port, Protocol, and Data Type values should appear naturally rendered within the GUI's dropdowns and text fields, not pasted as plain text overlays.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires configuring the OpenBCI GUI with Synthetic data. Stream 1 must be set to Protocol: LSL, Data Type: TimeSeries. Stream 2 must be set to Protocol: OSC, Data Type: BandPower, IP: 127.0.0.1, Port: 12345. The network streams must be started. Finally, a screenshot must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots.
